name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions:
1. Bar on upper traps, feet shoulder-width
2. Brace core, bend knees and hips
3. Lower until thighs parallel
4. Drive through heels to stand
tips: Keep chest up. Don't let knees cave inward.

name: Front Squat
instructions:
1. Bar on front delts, elbows high
2. Feet shoulder-width, brace core
3. Squat down keeping torso upright
4. Drive up through heels
tips: Keep elbows high throughout. More quad-dominant than back squat.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbell at chest level
2. Feet shoulder-width apart
3. Squat down between knees
4. Drive up to standing
tips: Great for learning squat pattern. Keep dumbbell close to chest.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions:
1. Rear foot on bench behind you
2. Hold dumbbells at sides
3. Lower until front thigh parallel
4. Push through front heel to rise
tips: Keep torso upright. Front knee tracks over toes.

name: Leg Press
instructions:
1. Sit in machine, feet shoulder-width on platform
2. Release safety, lower weight
3. Bend knees to 90 degrees
4. Press platform away
tips: Don't lock knees at top. Keep lower back flat against pad.

name: Hack Squat
instructions:
1. Stand on platform, shoulders under pads
2. Release safety handles
3. Bend knees to lower
4. Press up through heels
tips: Excellent quad isolation. Vary foot placement for emphasis.

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions:
1. Feet hip-width, bar over mid-foot
2. Hinge at hips, grip bar
3. Flatten back, chest up
4. Drive through floor to stand
5. Lock out at top
tips: Keep bar close to body. Never round lower back.

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions:
1. Wide stance, toes pointed out
2. Grip bar between legs
3. Chest up, hips low
4. Drive through heels to stand
tips: Push knees out over toes. More glute/adductor focused than conventional.

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions:
1. Hold bar at hip height
2. Slight knee bend, push hips back
3. Lower bar along thighs
4. Drive hips forward to return
tips: Feel the hamstring stretch. Keep bar close to legs.

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
instructions:
1. Stand inside trap bar
2. Grip handles, hips back
3. Chest up, drive through floor
4. Stand tall, lock out
tips: More quad involvement than conventional. Easier on the lower back.

name: Bench Press
instructions:
1. Lie flat, eyes under bar
2. Grip wider than shoulders
3. Unrack, lower to mid-chest
4. Press up to lockout
tips: Pinch shoulder blades. Use a spotter for heavy sets.

name: Incline Bench Press
instructions:
1. Set bench to 30-45 degrees
2. Grip bar wider than shoulders
3. Lower to upper chest
4. Press up to lockout
tips: Targets upper chest. Don't flare elbows too wide.

name: Decline Bench Press
instructions:
1. Set bench to decline
2. Hook feet under pads
3. Lower bar to lower chest
4. Press up to lockout
tips: Targets lower chest fibers. Use a spotter.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions:
1. Lie flat, dumbbells at chest level
2. Press up, bringing weights together
3. Lower with control
4. Feel chest stretch at bottom
tips: Greater range of motion than barbell. Squeeze at top.

name: Dumbbell Flyes
instructions:
1. Lie flat, arms extended above chest
2. Slight bend in elbows
3. Lower arms in arc to sides
4. Squeeze chest to return
tips: Don't go too heavy. Focus on the stretch and squeeze.

name: Cable Crossover
instructions:
1. Set pulleys to high position
2. Step forward, slight lean
3. Bring handles together in front
4. Squeeze chest, return slowly
tips: Great isolation exercise. Constant tension throughout.

name: Push-Up
instructions:
1. Hands shoulder-width, body straight
2. Lower chest to floor
3. Push up to full extension
4. Keep core tight throughout
tips: Scale by doing knee push-ups or elevating hands.

name: Overhead Press
instructions:
1. Bar on front shoulders
2. Brace core, press overhead
3. Lock out arms
4. Lower with control
tips: Squeeze glutes for stability. Move head back as bar passes face.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions:
1. Sit or stand, dumbbells at shoulders
2. Press overhead
3. Lock out at top
4. Lower with control
tips: Don't arch your back excessively. Full range of motion.

name: Arnold Press
instructions:
1. Dumbbells at chest, palms facing you
2. Rotate palms outward as you press up
3. Lock out overhead, palms forward
4. Reverse the rotation on the way down
tips: Smooth rotation throughout the press. Hits all three delt heads.

name: Lateral Raise
instructions:
1. Stand with dumbbells at sides
2. Raise arms to sides until parallel
3. Slight bend in elbows
4. Lower slowly
tips: Don't swing. Lead with elbows. Light weight, high reps.

name: Front Raise
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells in front of thighs
2. Raise one or both arms to eye level
3. Keep arms straight
4. Lower with control
tips: Avoid swinging. Alternate arms if fatiguing quickly.

name: Rear Delt Fly
instructions:
1. Hinge forward at hips
2. Arms hanging down
3. Raise arms to sides, squeezing rear delts
4. Lower slowly
tips: Keep slight bend in elbows. Squeeze shoulder blades together.

name: Upright Row
instructions:
1. Hold bar with narrow grip
2. Pull bar up to chin level
3. Lead with elbows
4. Lower with control
tips: Wide grip is safer for shoulders. Don't go above chin level.

name: Barbell Shrug
instructions:
1. Hold barbell at arm's length
2. Shrug shoulders straight up
3. Hold at top 1-2 seconds
4. Lower slowly
tips: Don't roll shoulders. Straight up and down only.

name: Barbell Row
instructions:
1. Hinge forward, back flat
2. Grip bar wider than shoulders
3. Pull to lower ribcage
4. Squeeze shoulder blades at top
tips: Don't use momentum. Pause at top for max contraction.

name: Dumbbell Row
instructions:
1. One knee and hand on bench
2. Row dumbbell to hip
3. Squeeze lat at top
4. Lower with control
tips: Keep back flat. Drive elbow past torso.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions:
1. Sit with feet on pads
2. Grip handle, arms extended
3. Pull handle to stomach
4. Squeeze shoulder blades
5. Return slowly
tips: Don't lean back excessively. Focus on squeezing back muscles.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions:
1. Grip bar wider than shoulders
2. Sit with thighs under pad
3. Pull bar to upper chest
4. Squeeze lats, return slowly
tips: Don't lean back too far. Pull with your elbows, not hands.

name: Pull-Up
instructions:
1. Grip bar wider than shoulders
2. Hang with arms extended
3. Pull chin above bar
4. Lower with control
tips: Use band assistance if needed. Full range of motion.

name: Chin-Up
instructions:
1. Grip bar with palms facing you
2. Hang with arms extended
3. Pull chin above bar
4. Lower with control
tips: More bicep emphasis than pull-ups. Great compound movement.

name: T-Bar Row
instructions:
1. Straddle the bar
2. Grip handle, chest forward
3. Pull to chest
4. Squeeze at top, lower slowly
tips: Keep back flat. Great for mid-back thickness.

name: Face Pull
instructions:
1. Set cable at face height
2. Grip rope with overhand
3. Pull toward face, spreading rope
4. Externally rotate at end
tips: Great for shoulder health and posture. Light weight, high reps.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions:
1. Stand with bar at arm's length
2. Curl bar up to shoulders
3. Squeeze biceps at top
4. Lower with control
tips: Don't swing. Keep elbows pinned to sides.

name: Dumbbell Curl
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells at sides
2. Curl up, supinating wrists
3. Squeeze at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Alternate or do both together. Control the negative.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells with neutral grip
2. Curl up keeping palms facing in
3. Squeeze at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Hits the brachioradialis. Great for forearm development.

name: Preacher Curl
instructions:
1. Rest arms on preacher pad
2. Grip EZ-bar
3. Curl up to shoulders
4. Lower with full control
tips: Eliminates momentum. Great for bicep peak.

name: Concentration Curl
instructions:
1. Sit, elbow on inner thigh
2. Curl dumbbell up
3. Squeeze hard at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Focus on the squeeze. Excellent for mind-muscle connection.

name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions:
1. Grip bar or rope at cable
2. Elbows pinned to sides
3. Push down to full extension
4. Return slowly
tips: Don't flare elbows. Squeeze at bottom.

name: Skull Crushers
instructions:
1. Lie on bench, bar overhead
2. Bend elbows, lower bar to forehead
3. Extend arms to press up
4. Keep upper arms vertical
tips: Control the weight. Don't let elbows flare.

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbell overhead with both hands
2. Lower behind head by bending elbows
3. Extend arms to press up
4. Keep elbows close to ears
tips: Stretches the long head. Control the weight.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions:
1. Lie on bench, grip narrower than shoulders
2. Lower bar to lower chest
3. Press up, focusing on triceps
4. Lock out at top
tips: Hands about 14 inches apart. Great compound tricep builder.

name: Dip
instructions:
1. Support yourself on dip bars
2. Lower body by bending elbows
3. Go to 90 degrees or below
4. Press back up
tips: Lean forward for chest focus. Stay upright for tricep focus.

name: Dumbbell Lunge
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells at sides
2. Step forward with one leg
3. Lower until both knees at 90 degrees
4. Push through front heel
tips: Keep torso upright. Alternate legs.

name: Walking Lunge
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells at sides
2. Step forward into lunge
3. Drive up and step into next lunge
4. Continue walking pattern
tips: Keep upright posture. Long strides for glutes, short for quads.

name: Reverse Lunge
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells at sides
2. Step backward into lunge
3. Lower until front thigh parallel
4. Push through front heel to return
tips: Easier on knees than forward lunge. Great for beginners.

name: Step-Up
instructions:
1. Stand facing bench with dumbbells
2. Step up with one foot
3. Drive through heel to stand on bench
4. Step down with control
tips: Don't push off back foot. Let working leg do the work.

name: Leg Extension
instructions:
1. Sit in machine, pad above ankles
2. Extend legs to straight
3. Squeeze quads at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Don't lock knees violently. Control the negative.

name: Leg Curl
instructions:
1. Lie face down, pad behind ankles
2. Curl legs up toward glutes
3. Squeeze hamstrings at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Don't let hips lift. Full range of motion.

name: Hip Thrust
instructions:
1. Upper back on bench, bar over hips
2. Feet flat, shoulder-width
3. Drive hips up, squeezing glutes
4. Hold at top, lower slowly
tips: Best glute isolation exercise. Squeeze hard at top.

name: Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Lie on back, knees bent
2. Drive hips up toward ceiling
3. Squeeze glutes at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Pause at top for 2 seconds. Great glute activation.

name: Calf Raise
instructions:
1. Stand on edge of step
2. Lower heels below platform
3. Rise up on toes
4. Squeeze at top
tips: Full range of motion. Slow negatives for growth.

name: Seated Calf Raise
instructions:
1. Sit with pad on knees
2. Lower heels
3. Push up on toes
4. Squeeze at top
tips: Targets the soleus muscle. Pair with standing raises.

name: Good Morning
instructions:
1. Bar on upper back like a squat
2. Slight knee bend
3. Hinge forward at hips
4. Return to upright
tips: Light weight to start. Great posterior chain exercise.

name: Chest Supported Row
instructions:
1. Lie face down on incline bench
2. Dumbbells hanging below
3. Row to chest level
4. Squeeze shoulder blades
tips: Eliminates cheating. Great isolation for back.

name: Pendlay Row
instructions:
1. Bar on floor, hinged over
2. Back parallel to floor
3. Explosively row to chest
4. Return bar to floor each rep
tips: Each rep starts from floor. Builds explosive back strength.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions:
1. Lie on bench, hold dumbbell overhead
2. Lower behind head in arc
3. Feel stretch in lats/chest
4. Pull back over chest
tips: Keep slight elbow bend. Great for rib cage expansion.

name: Wrist Curl
instructions:
1. Forearms on bench, wrists over edge
2. Curl bar up with wrists
3. Squeeze at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Light weight, high reps. Builds grip strength.

name: Reverse Wrist Curl
instructions:
1. Forearms on bench, palms down
2. Extend wrists upward
3. Lower slowly
4. Keep forearms still
tips: Balances forearm development. Prevents imbalances.

name: Landmine Press
instructions:
1. Hold end of barbell at shoulder
2. Press up and forward
3. Lock out at top
4. Lower with control
tips: Shoulder-friendly pressing angle. Great for overhead mobility issues.

name: Landmine Row
instructions:
1. Stand over barbell end
2. Grip with one or both hands
3. Row to chest
4. Lower with control
tips: Unique angle for back development. Try one-arm variation.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions:
1. Set cable at lowest position
2. Stand sideways to machine
3. Raise arm to side
4. Lower with control
tips: Constant tension from cable. Better than dumbbells for isolation.

name: Machine Chest Press
instructions:
1. Sit with back against pad
2. Grip handles at chest level
3. Press forward to extension
4. Return slowly
tips: Great for beginners. Controlled movement path.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions:
1. Sit with back supported
2. Grip handles at shoulder level
3. Press up to extension
4. Lower with control
tips: Safe for beginners. Focus on squeezing delts.

name: Pec Deck Fly
instructions:
1. Sit with arms on pads
2. Bring pads together in front
3. Squeeze chest
4. Return slowly
tips: Don't go too far back. Controlled squeeze at the front.

name: Reverse Pec Deck
instructions:
1. Face the machine
2. Grip handles with arms extended
3. Pull handles apart
4. Squeeze rear delts
tips: Great for posture. Light weight, feel the contraction.

name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions:
1. Bar on upper traps in smith machine
2. Feet slightly forward
3. Squat down to parallel
4. Press up through heels
tips: Feet forward targets quads more. Guided bar path for safety.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions:
1. Stand on one leg, dumbbell in opposite hand
2. Hinge forward, rear leg extends back
3. Lower until torso parallel
4. Drive hips forward to return
tips: Great for balance and unilateral strength. Go slow.

name: Cable Pull-Through
instructions:
1. Face away from low cable
2. Grip rope between legs
3. Hinge at hips, let cable pull back
4. Drive hips forward to stand
tips: Great glute exercise. Squeeze hard at the top.

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions:
1. Sit on incline bench, arms hanging
2. Curl dumbbells up
3. Squeeze at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Greater stretch on bicep. Don't swing.

name: Cable Curl
instructions:
1. Grip cable bar at low pulley
2. Curl up to shoulders
3. Squeeze at top
4. Lower slowly
tips: Constant tension throughout. Great for pump.

name: Tricep Kickback
instructions:
1. Hinge forward, upper arm parallel
2. Extend forearm back
3. Squeeze tricep at lockout
4. Lower slowly
tips: Keep upper arm still. Light weight, squeeze hard.

name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions:
1. Hands together under chest in diamond shape
2. Lower chest to hands
3. Push up to extension
4. Keep elbows close to body
tips: Great tricep exercise. Scale with knees if needed.

name: Farmer's Walk
instructions:
1. Pick up heavy dumbbells
2. Stand tall, shoulders back
3. Walk with purpose
4. Maintain posture throughout
tips: Great for grip and overall conditioning. Keep chest up.

name: Deficit Deadlift
instructions:
1. Stand on 2-4 inch platform
2. Set up like conventional deadlift
3. Extended range of motion from deficit
4. Drive through to lockout
tips: Builds strength off the floor. Use less weight than regular deadlift.

name: Pause Squat
instructions:
1. Squat down to parallel
2. Pause for 2-3 seconds at bottom
3. Drive up explosively
4. Lockout at top
tips: Eliminates stretch reflex. Builds strength out of the hole.

name: Zercher Squat
instructions:
1. Hold barbell in crook of elbows
2. Squat down keeping torso upright
3. Drive up through heels
4. Maintain bar position
tips: Builds incredible core strength. Use padding on bar.

name: Floor Press
instructions:
1. Lie on floor, barbell above chest
2. Lower until triceps touch floor
3. Pause briefly
4. Press up to lockout
tips: Reduces shoulder stress. Great for lockout strength.

name: Spoto Press
instructions:
1. Lower bar to 1 inch above chest
2. Pause for 1-2 seconds
3. Press up to lockout
4. Maintain tension throughout
tips: Builds chest strength without bounce. Use a spotter.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions:
1. Hold dumbbells in front of thighs
2. Hinge at hips, slight knee bend
3. Lower along thighs
4. Drive hips forward to return
tips: More wrist-friendly than barbell version. Feel the stretch.

name: Sissy Squat
instructions:
1. Hold onto support for balance
2. Rise on toes, lean back
3. Bend knees deeply, leaning further back
4. Return to start
tips: Intense quad isolation. Hold support for safety.

name: Leg Press Calf Raise
instructions:
1. Feet on bottom of leg press platform
2. Only balls of feet on platform
3. Push through toes
4. Lower heels below platform
tips: Full range of motion. Don't lock legs completely.

name: Rack Pull
instructions:
1. Set bar at knee height in rack
2. Grip bar, chest up
3. Drive hips forward to lockout
4. Lower to pins
tips: Overload top portion of deadlift. Great for back thickness.

name: Meadows Row
instructions:
1. Stand perpendicular to landmine
2. Grip end of bar with overhand
3. Row to hip
4. Lower with stretch
tips: Named after John Meadows. Great lat stretch and contraction.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions:
1. Set bench to 30-45 degrees
2. Press dumbbells up from shoulders
3. Bring together at top
4. Lower with control
tips: Great upper chest builder. Don't bounce at bottom.

name: Inverted Row
instructions:
1. Set bar at waist height
2. Hang underneath, body straight
3. Pull chest to bar
4. Lower with control
tips: Great pull-up progression. Elevate feet for harder variation.

name: Hip Abduction Machine
instructions:
1. Sit in machine, legs together
2. Push knees apart against pads
3. Squeeze at full open position
4. Return slowly
tips: Great for glute medius. Control the return.

name: Hip Adduction Machine
instructions:
1. Sit in machine, legs apart
2. Squeeze knees together
3. Hold at closed position
4. Return slowly
tips: Important for injury prevention. Don't rush.

name: Glute Ham Raise
instructions:
1. Lock feet in GHD
2. Start with torso upright
3. Lower body forward under control
4. Curl back up using hamstrings
tips: Very challenging. Use band assistance if needed.

name: Back Extension
instructions:
1. Lock feet, thighs on pad
2. Lower torso toward floor
3. Raise back to straight
4. Don't hyperextend
tips: Strengthen lower back. Hold weight plate for added resistance.

name: Cable Woodchop
instructions:
1. Set cable high
2. Grip handle with both hands
3. Rotate torso pulling down and across
4. Control the return
tips: Rotate through the torso, not arms. Great rotational strength.
